evidence_quotes.quote: personalised digital experiences.

paragraphs.text: At Adobe, our mission is to change the world through personalised digital experiences. Since our founding over 40 years ago, we have pioneered transformative technologies that allow our customers—who range from emerging artists to global brands—to channel their imaginations, unleash their creativity, and power their businesses.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: Our submission will focus on subscription services.

paragraphs.text: The European Union is Adobe's second-largest market outside of the United States. We have been operating here for nearly 40 years and employ over 3300 individuals across 14 EU Member States, from Ireland to Romania. Adobe welcomes the opportunity to contribute to the call for evidence issued by the European Commission's Directorate-General for Justice and Consumers regarding the proposed Digital Fairness Act. Our submission will focus on subscription services.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: Since the introduction of our subscription model in 2013
our subscription model lowered barriers for students, individuals, and small businesses—empowering them to compete alongside larger enterprises.

paragraphs.text: Since the introduction of our subscription model in 2013, we have sought to make cutting-edge creativity tools more accessible, affordable, and secure. Boxed software represented a significant up-front cost for our customers. By moving away from perpetual licenses and physical distribution, our subscription model lowered barriers for students, individuals, and small businesses—empowering them to compete alongside larger enterprises. This shift also enabled the launch of cloud-based services, continuous product updates, and stronger security protections, while keeping pace with the broader digital transformation of society, for example the rapidly increasing demand for creative digital content.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: subscription model
different plans/pricing/levels of flexibility and commitment
simple and straightforward option to cancel
renewal reminders

paragraphs.text: Adobe created its subscription model with a set of principles in mind: it's all about consumer choice (different plans/pricing/levels of flexibility and commitment); consumer experience (easy access to all relevant information including a simple and straightforward option to cancel); and transparent communication at all stages (prior to buying, renewal reminders, and cancellation).

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: Subscriptions are essential to ensuring customers have access to the latest AI innovations at the pace at which they are being developed.

paragraphs.text: Today, generative AI is driving the next wave of innovation, reshaping how people create and work. Subscriptions are essential to ensuring customers have access to the latest AI innovations at the pace at which they are being developed.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

paragraphs.text: In this submission, we set out our view on some of the key points raised in the European Commission's consultation questionnaire. We share insights on consumer behaviour and our commercial practices and reference a recent survey of EU consumers published by the Business Software Alliance.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: prefer subscriptions to one-time purchases.

paragraphs.text: Given the choice, 65% of consumers across markets prefer subscriptions to one-time purchases. The subscriptions model has great perceived value for consumers who point to the following benefits:

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: flexibility to cancel a subscription

paragraphs.text: flexibility to cancel a subscription (93% agreed);

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

paragraphs.text: convenience and ease of access (92% agreed);

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: flexible price options (89% agreed);

paragraphs.text: flexible price options (89% agreed);

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

paragraphs.text: flexible durations (89% agreed);

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

paragraphs.text: continuous and automatic security updates (88% agreed).

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: to offer transparent and responsible subscriptions

paragraphs.text: For certain industries, the model is trusted by consumers. On this point, not all subscription services are seen as the same, and not all kinds of subscription companies are accorded the same level of trust. Technology software companies, specifically, are much more trusted than others to offer transparent and responsible subscriptions, earning levels of trust that are 64% higher than for social media companies and 48% higher than for gaming companies. As the European Commission seeks to address areas of concern, it is important to keep in mind the value consumers derive from subscriptions services and that certain industries have established trust levels that are significantly higher than others.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: the wide diversity of subscriptions services,

paragraphs.text: Additionally, fewer consumers (34%) indicate the need for greater regulation of their purchasing decisions. Given this and the wide diversity of subscriptions services, the focus should be on the enforcement of existing law to pursue bad actors, while preserving the significant benefits subscription models have brought to consumers.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

paragraphs.text: The European Commission should therefore adopt a principles-based approach. By contrast, an overly prescriptive or simplistic approach would risk having perverse and unintended effects on consumers. Notably, taken together the results of the BSA survey support the current definition of a consumer as "someone who is reasonably well-informed, observant and circumspect".

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: consumers should have an easy functionality (such as a cancellation link or a button) on the trader's interface to exercise their right to cancel the online contract.

paragraphs.text: The European Commission wishes to understand whether "consumers should have an easy functionality (such as a cancellation link or a button) on the trader's interface to exercise their right to cancel the online contract."

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: straightforward cancellation functionality

paragraphs.text: A full 85% of consumers agree that they should have straightforward cancellation functionality such as a link or button within their account settings; Adobe agrees wholeheartedly and already provides such a functionality. Adobe also believes that a simple and straightforward cancellation process must also ensure that customers receive the information they need.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: 69% of consumers fear losing access to their stored preferences or content upon cancellation.
81% agree they should be informed of any potential data loss before cancellation.
Adobe's customers need and expect to be informed about how to avoid the loss of their creations upon cancellation.

paragraphs.text: 64% of consumers also agree that subscriptions such as media streaming are fundamentally different from subscriptions for digital tools and software. Clearly the products are not the same but furthermore the customer relationship is not the same; the responsibility is not the same. Adobe is often trusted with storing our customers' entire creative portfolios – a responsibility we do not take lightly. We know that 69% of consumers fear losing access to their stored preferences or content upon cancellation. Also 81% agree they should be informed of any potential data loss before cancellation. Adobe's customers need and expect to be informed about how to avoid the loss of their creations upon cancellation.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: when customers enter the cancellation flow
74% of consumers support the idea of being offered a discount or the possibility to adapt their offer to their evolving needs when they begin the cancellation process.

paragraphs.text: Furthermore, when customers enter the cancellation flow, they may also be seeking solutions that better fit their current needs or opportunities to adjust their account. 74% of consumers support the idea of being offered a discount or the possibility to adapt their offer to their evolving needs when they begin the cancellation process. In the last year alone, approximately 230 000 Adobe customers have adapted their offer in this way.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: reasons for cancelling a subscription

paragraphs.text: The cancellation flow is also an opportunity for consumers to offer their opinion on how services can be improved. 65% of consumers surveyed supported being asked to provide feedback on the reasons for cancelling a subscription to help improve the offering.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: cancellation processes be "simple and straightforward"

paragraphs.text: It is therefore important to allow sufficient flexibility in the cancellation flow to ensure that consumers remain informed, protected, and in control. The EU can achieve the goal of protecting consumers by recommending that cancellation processes be "simple and straightforward".

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

paragraphs.text: iii. Transparency

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: always be reminded before their subscription is automatically renewed
a free trial is converted into a paid subscription

paragraphs.text: The European Commission wishes to understand whether: "consumers should benefit from more transparency, e.g. always be reminded before their subscription is automatically renewed or a free trial is converted into a paid subscription".

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

paragraphs.text: Adobe agrees that transparency is crucial. It requires a multi-pronged approach, including relevant pre-contractual information, clear confirmations, and effective reminders.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

paragraphs.text: In terms of pre-contractual information, consumers prefer this to be short and focused on the key information that drives their purchasing decision:

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: Information about how much they will be charged, and when

paragraphs.text: Information about how much they will be charged, and when (looked for by 60%)

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: Information about how to cancel the subscription

paragraphs.text: Information about how to cancel the subscription (looked for by 55%)

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: how long they have to cancel the subscription

paragraphs.text: Information about how long they have to cancel the subscription and get a refund (looked for by 49%)

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

paragraphs.text: Confirmation emails should repeat this type of key information.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: too much communication from a subscription service

paragraphs.text: With regard to reminders, too much communication from a subscription service can lead to diminishing impact and can damage the customer experience.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: Over nine-in-ten consumers (94%) feel they get too many or the right number of emails regarding their subscriptions.

paragraphs.text: Over nine-in-ten consumers (94%) feel they get too many or the right number of emails regarding their subscriptions.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: reminder one month before a contract renews

paragraphs.text: 49% of consumers indicate a preference for a reminder one month before a contract renews – only 8% say they expect a reminder 6 months out.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: trials, conversions, and notice periods

paragraphs.text: iv. Control over contracts: trials, conversions, and notice periods

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: possibility to terminate an automatically extended digital subscription any time with a short notice (e.g. one month) or by having to approve explicitly the renewal of a subscription or the conversion of a free trial into a paid subscription

paragraphs.text: The European Commission wishes to understand whether consumers have sufficient control over their contracts. Specifically, it raises the "possibility to terminate an automatically extended digital subscription any time with a short notice (e.g. one month) or by having to approve explicitly the renewal of a subscription or the conversion of a free trial into a paid subscription".

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: Requirements for additional, separate opt-in consent for trial conversions need to take into account trial length.

For short trials, of say less than a month, it makes little sense and creates unnecessary friction in the customer journey to ask for trial conversion consent, already given when agreeing to the trial, to be repeated.
However, longer free trials may create too significant a gap between the trial sign-up process and the purchase commitment.
In this case, additional consent may be warranted.

paragraphs.text: 61% of consumers indicate they benefit from free trials. Requirements for additional, separate opt-in consent for trial conversions need to take into account trial length. For short trials, of say less than a month, it makes little sense and creates unnecessary friction in the customer journey to ask for trial conversion consent, already given when agreeing to the trial, to be repeated. However, longer free trials may create too significant a gap between the trial sign-up process and the purchase commitment. In this case, additional consent may be warranted.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: trial length
free trials

paragraphs.text: An approach, which takes into account trial length is all the more important to avoid interrupting services, lengthening sign-up processes and complicating subscription offerings. Furthermore, experience in other jurisdictions has also shown that some companies cease offering free trials to avoid complicating their customer flows, which might be an unintended consequence of new legislative trial requirements.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: free trials

paragraphs.text: More generally, so that consumers are protected, Adobe ensures that free trials:

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: include the length of contract, trial period, or promotional pricing terms at the point of subscription;

paragraphs.text: include the length of contract, trial period, or promotional pricing terms at the point of subscription;

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: prominently display the limited duration of the free trial in the shopping cart and throughout the purchase experience;

paragraphs.text: prominently display the limited duration of the free trial in the shopping cart and throughout the purchase experience;

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: for longer trials (more than one week), have reminder emails to ensure customers are aware when the trial period is ending.

For shorter periods (7 days or fewer), reminder emails can be seen as superfluous to the confirmation email, which gives details of the trial; according to the survey, only 6% of consumers believe they receive too few emails.

paragraphs.text: for longer trials (more than one week), have reminder emails to ensure customers are aware when the trial period is ending. For shorter periods (7 days or fewer), reminder emails can be seen as superfluous to the confirmation email, which gives details of the trial; according to the survey, only 6% of consumers believe they receive too few emails.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: Adobe agrees that it should be simple to terminate an automatically extended digital subscription at any time with a short notice (see cancellations).
A fee to recover costs for early termination allows companies to maintain this type of (discounted) offer.

paragraphs.text: Adobe agrees that it should be simple to terminate an automatically extended digital subscription at any time with a short notice (see cancellations). At the same time, consumers indicate a strong preference (60%) for paying less for a longer-term commitment, as opposed to paying more for a subscription that can be cancelled any time, but at a higher monthly price (40%). A fee to recover costs for early termination allows companies to maintain this type of (discounted) offer. This fee should, of course, be reasonable.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

paragraphs.text: v. Communication with a human

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: consumers should have a right to request to communicate with a person in case of a problem with their contract, not only an automated chatbot

paragraphs.text: The European Commission wishes to understand whether "consumers should have a right to request to communicate with a person in case of a problem with their contract, not only an automated chatbot".

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: the option to speak to a human agent is important

paragraphs.text: Adobe agrees that the option to speak to a human agent is important and offers this option.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

paragraphs.text: vi. Automated contracting

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: Automated contracting refers to the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) for autonomous conclusion of contracts, such as via digital assistants or smart devices.

paragraphs.text: The European Commission wishes to understand whether: "specific measures should be taken to protect consumers online in the context of automated contracting (Automated contracting refers to the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) for autonomous conclusion of contracts, such as via digital assistants or smart devices.)"

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

paragraphs.text: The relevant provisions of the EU AI Act and the soon to be developed associated guidelines and Code of Practice should provide the necessary transparency.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: The subscriptions model has been overwhelmingly positive for consumers of software services.

paragraphs.text: The subscriptions model has been overwhelmingly positive for consumers of software services. Consumers have a clear preference for subscriptions models over one-time purchases (65%), whilst they are fewer (34%) to indicate the need for greater regulation of their purchasing decisions. Certain industries have also established trust levels that are significantly higher than others. The focus should therefore be on the enforcement of existing law to pursue bad actors, while preserving the significant benefits subscription models have brought to consumers.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: At the heart of a positive relationship between subscription services providers and consumers are choice, consumer experience and transparent communication.

paragraphs.text: At the heart of a positive relationship between subscription services providers and consumers are choice, consumer experience and transparent communication. Being overly prescriptive risks having a negative effect on consumers in each of these areas. If the European Commission has specific and targeted areas of focus, where it intends to opt for a legislative solution, it is essential to adopt a principles-based approach.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: the tailored marketing materials we receive from our favorite brands

paragraphs.text: As one of the world's leading technology companies, Adobe is the engine behind the vast array of content we all consume across every facet of our lives: everything from the pitch decks and PDFs we review at work, to the creative posts and videos we see on social media, to award-winning movies; from the posters at our local coffee shops, to the billboards we all see on our commutes home, to the tailored marketing materials we receive from our favorite brands, and more.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

evidence_quotes.quote: AI Ethics principles of accountability, responsibility, and transparency

paragraphs.text: Adobe's business comprises a comprehensive technology suite that fuels both creativity and productivity. Across our product portfolio, Adobe is building on a decade-long legacy of responsible and ethical AI innovation by leveraging the power of AI to transform ideation, creation, and production. As we continue to harness the power of AI, we are committed to developing and deploying AI in line with our AI Ethics principles of accountability, responsibility, and transparency, while considering its broader impact on society.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

paragraphs.text: Adobe serves three audiences: business professionals and consumers, professional creatives and creators, students and marketing professionals.

section_path: Adobe Inc. Submission Digital Fairness Act European Commission (DG JUST) Call for Evidence

paragraphs.text: Business professionals and consumers want a quick and easy way to create standout content while getting their work done more efficiently and effectively. They do this through tools like Adobe Acrobat and our all-in-one visual creation tool Adobe Express.

Professional creatives and creators are looking to supercharge their skills with power and precision, automate their workflows, and unlock new creative ideation. They use tools like Illustrator and Photoshop to bring their creative visions to life while keeping up with the growing demand for content.

evidence_quotes.quote: Marketing professionals use Adobe Experience Platform to build personalised websites and ad campaigns while gaining deep customer insights in real time.

paragraphs.text: Marketing professionals use Adobe Experience Platform to build personalised websites and ad campaigns while gaining deep customer insights in real time. Most of the websites we see are powered by Adobe's marketing tools.
The contribution of the Central European Lawyers Initiative (CELI) is provided in the attached document.
